svg.title: Cross-lingual AMR Parsing Architecture
svg.desc: A five-stage pipeline: syntactic parsing, GNN graph encoding, isomorphic retrieval, optimal transport alignment, and autoregressive AMR decoding.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the target sentence input look like in cross-lingual AMR parsing?')
sendPrompt('How does the Stanza dependency parser work?')
sendPrompt('What is a syntactic dependency graph (V, E)?')
sendPrompt('What are XLM-R contextual embeddings and why 768 dimensions?')
sendPrompt('How does a GAT or GCN graph encoder work?')
sendPrompt('What are the target graph and node embeddings at 256 dimensions?')
sendPrompt('What is the AMR 3.0 corpus and why 1000 source graphs?')
sendPrompt('How does geodesic distance retrieval find structurally similar graphs?')
sendPrompt('What does the matched English AMR graph look like?')

svg.text: Target graph embeddings (from stage 2) + Matched AMR (from stage 3) feed the FGW solver →

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the Fused Gromov-Wasserstein solver and how does it align graphs?')
sendPrompt('What is the optimal transport coupling plan T and what information does it encode?')
sendPrompt('How are projected node embeddings computed from the transport plan?')
sendPrompt('What does the linear projection layer W_p do to the 256-dim embeddings?')
sendPrompt('How are prefix embeddings used to condition the AMRBART decoder?')
sendPrompt('What is AMRBART and why is its decoder frozen during training?')
